messages.text:
Gents, 

When I said I would work with Soylei, I had no idea how apparently dysfunctional some of the relationships are between some of you guys. Too many people complaining to me…like I’m some traffic cop on the playground regarding basic BS…all while you all have known each other for many, many years. Let me be clear, this business has no time for that, I certainly have no time for that. 

Growing a business is hard enough, you 6 owners need to get your shit together. Just so we are clear, when I ask for something to be done, it needs to be done promptly, in a few days, not weeks. Things like weekly priorities (top 3 simply stated). Take patents review - 60-70% of the patents need to be abandoned, in my view…saving the business perhaps $50-75k and allowing us to move on. This should have been done in a couple of days, discussed and than acted on. Prior development work, like Vitala+ need to be shelved…I’m not spending time, money effort on anything that doesn’t make commercial, financial and operational sense for this business in the near term. These are my calls, that’s what I laid out and you owners agreed to. 

I’ll be on the Monday morning call

My proposed 3 priorities are:
1. Qualifying products for use by local, state and federal agencies.
2. Building relationships for Soylei with companies. e.g. Inbra, Ingevity, Oldcastle, Hunt, Valero, InRoads, STATE Testing, Seneca, etc.
3. Helping to streamline product applications including customizing asphalt binder and mix formulations.
In terms of patents, keep all that are associated with RAFT including the molecules, method of making and applications. Surfactants and initiators should be kept. Certainly jettison the ATRP patents.
